kids.question: Who wrote the letter of 2 John?
kids.explanation: 2 John 1:1 — "The elder unto the elect lady and her children."

kids.question: What commandment does John say we should follow?
What did John say about people who deny Jesus came in the flesh?
kids.explanation: 2 John 1:7 — "many deceivers are entered into the world, who confess not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh. This is a deceiver and an antichrist."

teens.question: To whom is the letter of 2 John addressed?
teens.option_a: The elect lady and her children
teens.explanation: 2 John 1:1 — "The elder unto the elect lady and her children, whom I love in the truth."

teens.question: What does John say about the commandment to love one another?
teens.option_a: It is not a new commandment but one we had from the beginning
teens.option_b: It was just given by Jesus at the Last Supper
teens.option_d: It only applies to church leaders
teens.explanation: 2 John 1:5 — "not as though I wrote a new commandment unto thee, but that which we had from the beginning."

teens.question: What does John say walking in love means?
teens.explanation: 2 John 1:6 — "this is love, that we walk after his commandments."

teens.question: What does John warn about those who go beyond the doctrine of Christ?
teens.option_a: They do not have God; he that abides in the doctrine has both the Father and the Son
teens.option_b: They are the most advanced Christians
teens.explanation: 2 John 1:9 — "Whosoever transgresseth, and abideth not in the doctrine of Christ, hath not God."

teens.question: What does John say to do with someone who does not bring the doctrine of Christ?
teens.option_a: Do not receive him into your house or bid him God speed
teens.option_b: Welcome him warmly and debate him
teens.explanation: 2 John 1:10 — "If there come any unto you, and bring not this doctrine, receive him not into your house, neither bid him God speed."

adults.question: What is the significance of John calling himself "the elder" rather than "the apostle"?
adults.option_a: It emphasizes his pastoral authority and relationship rather than apostolic rank, suggesting a letter of personal care to a specific community
adults.option_b: He had lost his apostolic status
adults.option_c: He was too old to be called an apostle
adults.option_d: It was a required title for all church leaders
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:1 — "The elder" — John uses this intimate title showing pastoral relationship.

adults.question: What does John mean when he says he loves the elect lady "in the truth"?
adults.option_a: His love is grounded in and motivated by divine truth — not merely human affection but love rooted in the gospel
adults.option_c: He loves her more than others
adults.option_d: He is writing a formal legal document
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:1 — "whom I love in the truth; and not I only, but also all they that have known the truth."

adults.question: What specific heresy does John identify in verse 7?
adults.option_a: Denying that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh — an early form of Docetism or Gnostic teaching
adults.option_b: Denying the resurrection of the dead
adults.option_c: Teaching that there are many gods
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:7 — "many deceivers… who confess not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh. This is a deceiver and an antichrist."

adults.question: What does John say believers should "look to" regarding their work?
adults.option_a: "Look to yourselves, that we lose not those things which we have wrought, but that we receive a full reward"
adults.option_b: "Look to the heavens for signs of His coming"
adults.option_c: "Look to the poor and needy around you"
adults.option_d: "Look to the elders for guidance in all things"
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:8 — "Look to yourselves, that we lose not those things which we have wrought, but that we receive a full reward."

adults.question: What is the doctrinal test John gives for distinguishing true from false teachers?
adults.option_a: Whether they abide in the doctrine of Christ — those who transgress and do not abide in it have not God
adults.option_c: Whether they were trained by the apostles
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:9 — "Whosoever transgresseth, and abideth not in the doctrine of Christ, hath not God. He that abideth in the doctrine of Christ, he hath both the Father and the Son."

adults.question: What does "bid him God speed" mean in the context of verse 10, and why is it prohibited?
adults.option_a: Greeting a false teacher with blessing makes one a partaker of his evil deeds — it implies endorsement of his teaching
adults.option_b: It means wishing someone a safe journey
adults.option_c: It is a form of financial support
adults.option_d: It means praying for their conversion
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:11 — "he that biddeth him God speed is partaker of his evil deeds."

adults.question: Why does John say he chose not to write with paper and ink?
adults.option_a: He hopes to come and speak face to face, that their joy may be full
adults.option_b: He ran out of writing materials
adults.option_c: The subject was too sensitive for a letter
adults.option_d: He wanted Timothy to deliver the message verbally
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:12 — "I would not write with paper and ink: but I trust to come unto you, and speak face to face, that our joy may be full."

adults.question: Who sends greetings at the close of the letter?
adults.option_a: The children of the elect lady's sister
adults.explanation: 2 John 1:13 — "The children of thy elect sister greet thee."
